← all tutorials

TCP Handshake — interactive TCP_HANDSHAKE.md tcp_handshake.py

Step through the 3-way handshake, watch the sliding window fill and slide, and see the congestion sawtooth. The seq/ack arithmetic is recomputed live in JS from the identical constants as tcp_handshake.py (ISN_C=1000, ISN_S=5000, MSS=100, MSL=120).  |  Why Raft ignores TCP ordering →

① The 3-way handshake — each direction independently confirmed

What to do: click Next step to send SYN → SYN-ACK → ACK. Each flag consumes one sequence number, so a SYN at seq 1000 is acked with 1001. Both sides pick a fresh ISN so a stale segment from an old connection cannot land on the new sequence space.
step 0 / 3

② Sliding window — send 4, slide one on each ACK, keep the pipe full

What this shows: the sender may not have more than rwnd bytes in flight (flow control). The window fills at 4 segments, then slides right as each ACK confirms the oldest segment, letting one new segment leave. The pipeline stays full instead of send-one-wait-one.
in flight acked blocked (window full) window [send_base, send_base+rwnd)

③ Congestion control — slow start → AIMD → sawtooth on loss

What to do: drag ssthresh and the loss RTT, then watch the sawtooth reshape. cwnd doubles each RTT below ssthresh (exponential), grows +1/RTT above it (AIMD), and on loss: ssthresh=cwnd/2, cwnd=1. This re-runs the same simulate_congestion logic as tcp_handshake.py Section C.

④ Gold check — seq/ack arithmetic across the full connection lifecycle

These numbers are not made up — the page re-runs the seq/ack math in your browser on the same constants as tcp_handshake.py Gold Check. The green check: OK below confirms every value matches the .py to the integer.
check: … JS recompute matches tcp_handshake.py Gold Check (ack_of_syn=1001, ack_of_syn_ack=5001, data_ack=1401, FIN acks=1402/5002, TIME_WAIT=240s, cwnd sawtooth peak=19/ssthresh=9).
